clean_story:
Now I will teach you Sadness.

Sadness is like cold rain inside chest—no fire, no warmth, only heavy. Breath slow, body weak like after long hunger, but belly full of stone. Eyes leak water, not from smoke or wind, just drop without sound. Voice gone, like animal that stops howling when pack leaves. World turns gray like ash after big burn—nothing tastes, nothing calls. When heart sinks deep as sun in winter, that is Sadness.
